title: astragalus cerussatus
intro: Astragalus cerussatus, also known as the tinpot milkvetch or tinpot locoweed, is a species of flowering plant in the legume family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its adaptogenic and immune-boosting properties, with roots and stems being used to prepare medicinal preparations. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. Astragalus cerussatus is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also available in capsule or powder form for convenient herbal supplementation.
